message:
test(db): update identity FTS cleanup tests to reflect runtime stripping behavior

Update the identity FTS cleanup integration tests to match the new
runtime cleanup semantics introduced in the runtime-safe cleanup commit.

Test changes:
- Rename 'startup_runtime_keeps_message_fts_triggers' to
  'startup_runtime_strips_identity_fts_and_keeps_message_triggers'
- Rename 'startup_runtime_keeps_identity_fts_artifacts_with_migrations_disabled'
  to 'startup_runtime_strips_identity_fts_with_migrations_disabled'
- Update assertions: identity FTS artifacts (fts_agents, fts_projects)
  are now expected to be REMOVED at startup (count == 0)
- Preserve assertion that message FTS triggers remain intact (count == 3)
- Remove explicit run_migrations: false in one test (uses default)

These tests verify the critical invariant: runtime connections strip
corruption-prone identity FTS objects while preserving message indexing
triggers needed for ongoing server operation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
